methods.description: Process for collecting periphyton samples from different defined microhabitats within near-natural springs across Switzerland to determine species richness and community composition. Sampling was conducted during summer months from 2009 to 2011, with each spring sampled only once. The sampling area within each spring was delimited based on the spring type.

steps.description: We defined five different microhabitats that were sampled if present in each spring: stones, sediment, bryophytes, leaf litter, and filamentous green algae (Table S2).
steps.searchable_summary: Define microhabitat categories for sampling within springs
outputs: List of defined microhabitats (stones, sediment, bryophytes, leaf litter, filamentous green algae)

steps.description: We collected 175 samples in 71 springs. From every habitat, we sampled periphyton to determine species richness and community composition in a minimum of three different spots within a spring.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect periphyton samples from defined microhabitats in multiple spots per spring
inputs: Defined microhabitats present in each spring

steps.description: Sampling of the stone habitat was conducted by sampling 5-10 stones in each spring (depending on their size), or if only small gravel (ø < 2 cm) was available, 10-15 small stones were taken as a whole.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect stone or gravel samples from the stone microhabitat
inputs: Stones or gravel in stone microhabitat

steps.description: Sampling of the sediment habitat was conducted by taking circa 10-20 cm 3 of sediment from the surface.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect sediment samples from the sediment microhabitat
outputs: Sediment samples (approx. 10-20 cm³) with associated periphyton

steps.description: Bryophyte habitat, filamentous green-algae habitat, and leaf litter habitat was sampled by taking between 30-40 cm 3 of each habitat as a whole.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect samples from bryophyte, filamentous green algae, and leaf litter microhabitats
inputs: Bryophytes, filamentous green algae, or leaf litter in their respective microhabitats
outputs: Bryophyte samples (approx. 30-40 cm³) with associated periphyton
Filamentous green algae samples (approx. 30-40 cm³) with associated periphyton
Leaf litter samples (approx. 30-40 cm³) with associated periphyton

steps.description: Sampling was conducted during the summer months 2009-2011, and every spring was only sampled once.
steps.searchable_summary: Record sampling date and ensure single sampling event per spring

steps.description: In walled springs, rheocrenes, and rheohelocrenes, spring area was delimited where bryophyte canopies stopped growing. Helocrenes and hygropetric and linear springs were sampled on a stretch of maximal 3-5 times the spring mouth diameter, and the limnocrene was delimited by the pool's surface size.
steps.searchable_summary: Define spatial boundaries for sampling area based on spring type and characteristics
inputs: Spring type (walled, rheocrene, rheohelocrene, helocrene, hygropetric, linear, limnocrene)
Spring characteristics (bryophyte growth extent, spring mouth diameter, pool surface size)
outputs: Defined spatial sampling area for each spring
